subject: Project Management
title: Why Scrum Won
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Why Scrum Won.

hook.text: Dozens of Agile methods existed. Scrum captured over 80% of Agile adoption. Why?

buildup.text: XP required technical practices. Crystal was too nuanced. DSDM was too complex.

discovery.text: Scrum was framework-light: three roles, five events, three artifacts. Anyone could start Monday.

twist.text: The certification industry gave Scrum a massive distribution advantage — a trained army of evangelists.

climax.text: Scrum won not by being best, but by being easiest to adopt and most profitable to teach.

punchline.text: The simplest framework won the most complex contest.

quiz.question: What gave Scrum its adoption advantage over other Agile methods?
options: Simplicity and a large certification industry
